name: Mesenchymal Stem Cells
description: Mesenchymal stem cells (MSCs) from healthy donors improve cardiac function in experimental acute myocardial infarction (AMI) models. Osiris' stem cells are derived from human bone marrow. The source marrow is voluntarily donated by healthy adults between the ages of 18 and 30 years. Blood samples from the donor are screened prior to donation for transmissible diseases, including HIV and hepatitis, and the medical and social history of each donor is obtained to ascertain whether signs, symptoms or behaviors consistent with high risk for carrying a disease are present.
indication: Investigated for use/treatment in heart disease and myocardial infarction.
mechanismOfAction: Mesenchymal stem cells have a large capacity for self-renewal while maintaining their multipotency.
